one_liner: Front is a customer communication/helpdesk platform with a well-documented REST Core API supporting Bearer token and OAuth2 authentication, a free developer sandbox for building integrations, and an official MCP server for AI agent access to conversation data.

auth_methods.notes: Required for public integrations distributed via Front App Store; Basic auth used to exchange authorization code for tokens, then Bearer token used for API calls.
Personal/private API tokens can be generated directly in Front account settings for private integrations or testing; referred to as Bearer tokens.

self_serve_notes: Front offers a free developer account (separate from production, limited to 3 teammates) specifically for building and testing integrations without a paid subscription. Production usage requires a paid plan (no free production tier, but 14-day trial available).

api_surface.notes: Core API covers conversations, contacts, inboxes, tags, teams, accounts, drafts, attachments, and more, with OpenAPI spec available (llms.txt index).

evidence.supports: Describes Bearer token auth, API tokens vs OAuth2, and OAuth requirement for public integrations.
Shows self-serve API token generation and OAuth setup steps for building integrations, including free developer account link.
OAuth implementation guide for public integrations.
Reference to free developer account setup (#developer-accounts).

flags: Official MCP server exists at mcp.frontapp.com requiring OAuth 2.1 with PKCE, distinct from standard Core API OAuth2 flow.
Production API access tied to paid subscription plan; free tier only available via developer sandbox account.
No free production plan; 14-day trial only for paid tiers.
